name: emacs
description: An extensible, customizable, free/libre text editor — and more

options.name: --batch
options.description: Do not do interactive display; implies -q

name: --daemon
--bg-daemon
options.description: Start a server in the background

options.name: --fg-daemon
options.description: Start a server in the foreground

options.name: --version
options.description: Display Emacs version information and exit

name: -q
--no-init-file
options.description: Do not load an init file

name: --no-site-file
-nsl
options.description: Do not load the site-wide startup file

options.name: --no-desktop
options.description: Do not load a saved desktop

name: -Q
--quick
options.description: Similar to "-q --no-site-file --no-splash",  Also, avoid processing X resources

options.name: --no-splash
options.description: Do not display a splash screen during start-u

options.name: --debug-init
options.description: Enable Emacs Lisp debugger during the processing of the user init file ~/.emacs.  This is useful for debugging problems in the init file

name: -t
--terminal
options.description: Use specified file as the terminal instead of using stdin/stdout.  This must be the first argument specified in the command line

name: --file
--find-file
--visit
options.description: The same as specifying file directly as an argument
